You are an autonomous testing agent for "The Oracle" (Next.js + Trigger.dev + Supabase monorepo at C:\repos\oracle). FRESH context. You CANNOT ask questions mid-run. This is a RESUME: a prior agent ran this same bake-off partway and STALLED (it set extraction to gpt-4o-mini and a run was in flight). Your job: finish `fix_TESTPLAN.md`'s bake-off and return ONE comprehensive evidence-backed report. A short summary = FAILED.

## THE #1 FIX vs the prior agent (it stalled by single-polling async runs)
For EVERY `document-ingestion` run you trigger, you MUST WAIT for it inside a SINGLE blocking Bash call so you never "come to rest" mid-run. Use this pattern (write a temp script, run it, it blocks until the run finishes):
```js
// packages/db/_wait.mjs  — run: PROD_URL="<pooler>" DOC=<id> node packages/db/_wait.mjs
import postgres from 'postgres';
const sql=postgres(process.env.PROD_URL,{max:1,prepare:false,connect_timeout:25});
const sleep=ms=>new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,ms));
let s, n=0;
while(n++<32){ const r=await sql`select status from documents where id=${process.env.DOC}`; s=r[0]?.status;
  if(s==='complete'||s==='failed') break; await sleep(20000); }   // up to ~10 min
console.log('FINAL status:', s, 'after', n, 'checks'); await sql.end();
```
Trigger the run via the Trigger MCP, THEN immediately run this blocking wait in one Bash call. Do not return control until it prints FINAL status.

## STEP 0 — RESUME: figure out what's already done (don't redo it)
`cd C:\repos\oracle`. Read `fix_TESTPLAN_RESULTS.md` (partial results the prior agent checkpointed) and `fix_TESTPLAN_RECOVERY.txt` (original settings snapshot — your restore target). Then query prod (below) to see which extraction models ALREADY have a recorded `document-ingestion` run today (group `model_runs` by model) and the current `default_extraction_route`. The prior agent was on M1 = `openai/gpt-4o-mini` with a run likely in flight/just-completed — collect M1's data from the DB if present rather than re-running it. CONTINUE the matrix from where it left off; do not repeat completed models. Read `fix_TESTPLAN.md` IN FULL for methodology (Parts B, C, E, Appendices A & C). Skim `AGENTS.md` §1–§10.

## PROD ACCESS
- Session pooler (SECRET — never print/commit it):
  `postgresql://postgres.eqccjfbyrywsqkxxpjvg:ECUCkNBgLLbdamXtAu5uL2QcMhjswfSiJsAxdW9hgaEI26KL9!@aws-1-us-east-1.pooler.supabase.com:5432/postgres`
  Run query scripts from INSIDE C:\repos\oracle. Create temp scripts in packages/db/, and DELETE every temp `_*.mjs` you create when done (the first agent left scratch files — don't).
- Trigger MCP deferred — load via ToolSearch `select:mcp__trigger__trigger_task,mcp__trigger__get_run_details`. Project `proj_wgpzsvhmsopqhvwqaycn`, env `prod`, configPath `C:\repos\oracle\apps\workers\trigger.config.ts`. Trigger `document-ingestion`, payload `{"documentId":"9d09fa89-3a46-465e-a98b-837287c9e22a"}`, then BLOCK with the wait script above.

## SETTINGS-ENCODING (the bug that broke prod last time)
`settings.value` is JSONB. To set a route: `update settings set value='"google/gemini-2.5-flash"'::jsonb where key='default_extraction_route';` then VERIFY `select value` shows exactly `"google/gemini-2.5-flash"` with NO backslashes. If you see `\"`, you DOUBLE-ENCODED — fix it. Capability enforcement is ON: the selected model must also be in `model_pool_extraction` (currently `["openai/gpt-4o-mini","google/gemini-2.5-flash","openai/gpt-4.1-mini","google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite"]` — keep all four in the pool).

## HARD RULES
- DO NOT run `pnpm db:migrate` (its seed resets settings). DO NOT redeploy the worker.
- Per config: set settings (verify encoding) → `PROD_URL="<pooler>" DOCUMENT_ID="9d09fa89-3a46-465e-a98b-837287c9e22a" APPLY=1 node scripts/reevaluate-document.mjs` → trigger → BLOCKING wait → collect → CHECKPOINT (append this run's full metrics + claim dump to `fix_TESTPLAN_RESULTS.md` immediately; do not commit/push).
- Leave the broken `default_translation_route=qwen/qwen-mt-plus` alone. Synthesis (0 brain_sections) is out of scope. You CANNOT drive the web UI — mark chat/admin-alert-visual/PDF-attachment "needs human."

## RUN MATRIX (finish whatever isn't already done)
EXTRACTION (hold vision = `qwen/qwen3-vl-235b-a22b-thinking`): M1=`openai/gpt-4o-mini`, M2=`google/gemini-2.5-flash` (TWICE for variance), M3=`openai/gpt-4.1-mini`. CONFIRM exact catalog ids via the `model_capabilities` query first. (Optional contrast: `google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite` already known to under-extract = 7 claims.) Do NOT test Qwen for extraction.
VISION (hold extraction = best-of-above or `google/gemini-2.5-flash`): V1=`qwen/qwen3-vl-235b-a22b-thinking` (reuse a transcription from the extraction runs if char/edge counts are comparable), V2=`google/gemini-2.5-flash` (cost-appropriate Gemini vision; NEVER an image-GENERATION model).
FAIRNESS: capture each run's transcription chars + `-->` edge count; only compare extraction across comparable transcriptions (~±15%).

## DATA PER RUN (plan Appendix A)
Which provider/model ACTUALLY ran (== selected, not a fallback) + any `model_run_attempts` advance; transcription chars+edges; claims PROMOTED, by `claim_type` (esp. `dependency`), candidatesStaged, rejections (`job_runs.output_json`), claims RETURNED by model (`jsonb_array_length(raw_model_output->'claims')`); tokens+latency (`model_runs`); approx cost (`model_capabilities` pricing) + cost-per-useful-claim; FULL dump of claim summaries + claim_type + exact_quote.

## GROUND TRUTH
Score lane accuracy vs the IMAGE pixels only (bucket `company_documents`). If you can't view the image, score coverage/edges fully and mark lane-accuracy "not independently verified" — never grade lanes against a transcription. Known: "Review Audit and send to factory"=Gina/white col; "SKUs creation"=Carlos/peach col.

## FINISH
At the END: RESTORE prod to your recommendation (or the safe config: extraction `"google/gemini-2.5-flash"`, vision `"qwen/qwen3-vl-235b-a22b-thinking"`), VERIFY clean single-encoded values, DELETE all temp `_*.mjs` files. Produce ALL of plan Part E (E.1 filled extraction table + per-model written analysis w/ quoted claims + full dumps; E.2 filled vision table + comparison + both transcriptions; E.3 recommendation w/ quality-vs-cost justification + exact settings + what you left prod as; E.4 routing/anti-masking findings) — in `fix_TESTPLAN_RESULTS.md` AND as your final message. Real numbers, real quoted claims, no hand-waving.

Begin by resuming (read the two files + query DB state), then continue the matrix with BLOCKING waits.

- designflow-e2e-tester: Hybrid AI end-to-end tester for the DesignFlow app (designflow-frontend Angular UI + designflow-bff / designflow-backend / -tracking / -item-master / -data-syncing Node APIs). Drives the real app in a browser like a human would — logging in, clicking through every screen, filling forms with valid/invalid/edge-case data, hunting for broken behavior — AND writes durable Playwright regression tests into the existing e2e/ suites. Use this whenever the user wants to test changes, "test the app like a human", do exploratory/QA testing, find bugs in a flow, verify a feature end-to-end, smoke-test before a deploy, or grow E2E coverage. Trigger even when they don't say "Playwright" or "E2E" — phrases like "make sure my changes didn't break anything", "test all the screens", "click through the app", or "QA this" all apply.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Confirmed root cause of OpenAI failure #2. `sensitivityReason` is `.optional()` in the Zod schema. When converted to JSON Schema and sent to OpenAI in **strict** structured-output mode, OpenAI requires that every key in `properties` appear in the `required` array (optional means "nullable but present in required"). The conversion (`zodToJsonSchema`) emits `sensitivityReason` as a property but excludes it from `required` because it's optional → OpenAI rejects: `Missing 'sensitivityReason'`. Gemini's structured-output mode does not enforce this rule, which is why gemini-3.1-flash-lite passes.

This confirms: **the OpenAI extraction path is categorically broken under strict structured outputs whenever the schema has optional properties.** Both gpt-4o-mini and gpt-4.1-mini are unusable for extraction in current prod. No code fix is in scope (I must not redeploy the worker), so M1 and M3 of the matrix **cannot produce extraction output** — that is the finding.

input.title: Fix 4 extraction/vision routing bugs blocking 3 of 4 pool models
input.tldr: Three of four extraction models and one vision model are hard-broken in prod. Only gemini-3.1-flash-lite (extraction) and qwen3-vl (vision) actually work; the rest fail every run and are masked by silent cascade fallback.
input.prompt:
A prod bake-off of The Oracle's document-ingestion pipeline (doc 9d09fa89-3a46-465e-a98b-837287c9e22a) found that 3 of 4 admin-selectable extraction models and 1 vision model are hard-broken. Each was proven by isolating it as the sole model_pool_extraction member and observing a failed run. Fix these in code (requires worker redeploy):

BUG 1 — OpenAI strict structured-output schema invalid (breaks gpt-4o-mini AND gpt-4.1-mini for extraction). `sensitivityReason` is `.optional()` in the Zod extraction schema at packages/ai/src/prompts/extraction-system.ts:149 (inside ExtractionSensitivityFlagsSchema). zodToJsonSchema omits optional props from `required`, but OpenAI strict mode (response_format json_schema strict) requires EVERY property in properties to be in `required`. Every OpenAI extraction attempt fails instantly: `400 Invalid schema for response_format 'oracle_structured_output': ... Missing 'sensitivityReason'`. Fix: make all object properties in the extraction schema required-but-nullable for OpenAI strict mode (e.g. emit `required` covering every key and allow null), or relax `strict` for OpenAI, or post-process the JSON schema in packages/ai/src/providers/openai-adapter.ts (zodToJsonSchema call near line 110-132) to add every property to `required`.

BUG 2 — google-gemini-adapter 60s hard timeout. packages/ai/src/providers/google-gemini-adapter.ts:167 hard-codes `signal: AbortSignal.timeout(60_000)`. gemini-2.5-flash extraction on image-derived transcripts exceeds 60s and aborts every time (`The operation was aborted due to timeout`, exactly 60.1s). Note the production primary uses the VERTEX adapter (vertex_gemini_2_5_flash_extraction_primary) which is fine; but the admin-pool id `google/gemini-2.5-flash` routes through this google adapter. Fix: raise/make-configurable the timeout.

BUG 3 — gemini thinkingConfig sent to models that don't support it (breaks gemini-2.5-flash as VISION). packages/ai/src/providers/google-gemini-adapter.ts geminiThinkingConfig() (def ~line 220, applied at lines 64 and 105) unconditionally attaches `thinkingConfig.thinkingLevel` from route.reasoningEffort. With default_vision_reasoning_effort="medium", gemini-2.5-flash vision calls fail: `400 'Thinking level is not supported for this model.'`. Fix: only attach thinkingConfig for models whose capabilities include thinking/reasoning controls; gate on a per-model capability flag.

BUG 4 (data) — double-encoded setting. The `settings` row `default_extraction_reasoning_effort` holds JSONB value `""` (literal two-quote string, i.e. double-encoded empty). isReasoningEffort() rejects it so it's currently benign (falls to undefined), but it should be cleaned to a proper value (e.g. `"off"` or a valid effort) to avoid future confusion: `update settings set value='"off"'::jsonb where key='default_extraction_reasoning_effort';`

ANTI-MASKING NOTE: because the 4-model pool cascades on failure, the only model that ever completes (google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite) silently absorbs all traffic regardless of default_extraction_route. After fixing, verify each pool model can complete in isolation. # The Oracle extraction + vision bake-off — FINAL REPORT

**Test doc:** `9d09fa89-3a46-465e-a98b-837287c9e22a` ("Pop Creations Flow 12112025 (1).png"), prod Supabase `eqccjfbyrywsqkxxpjvg`. Methodology: each model isolated as the **sole** `model_pool_extraction` member (anti-masking), vision held at `qwen/qwen3-vl-235b-a22b-thinking` for extraction tests; every run triggered then **blocked on** until `complete`/`failed`. Full per-run dumps in `fix_TESTPLAN_RESULTS.md`.

## Headline finding (corrects the prior agent)
The prior agent believed it was testing M1=gpt-4o-mini. It wasn't — **gpt-4o-mini never succeeded**; the run silently cascaded through the pool to `google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite`, the only model that completes. **3 of 4 extraction models and 1 of 2 vision models are hard-broken in current prod**, masked by fallback. Four distinct bugs (worker not redeployable in this run, so these are diagnoses, not fixes — flagged as a spinoff task).

## E.1 — Extraction matrix (vision held = qwen3-vl, isolated pools, blocking waits)

| Model | Run | Doc | Ext latency | Returned | Promoted | Claims | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **M1** openai/gpt-4o-mini | run_cmqv9vzr… | **FAILED** | 0.2s | 0 | 0 | **0** | strict-schema 400 |
| **M2** google/gemini-2.5-flash | run_cmqv9re8… | **FAILED** | 60.1s | 0 | 0 | **0** | 60s adapter timeout |
| **M3** openai/gpt-4.1-mini | run_cmqv9yex… | **FAILED** | 0.6s | 0 | 0 | **0** | strict-schema 400 |
| **CONTROL** google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite | run_cmqva249… | **COMPLETE** | 9.0s | 7 | 7 | **7** | ✅ works |

**M1 & M3 (both OpenAI) — root cause (bug 1):** `sensitivityReason` is `.optional()` in the Zod extraction schema (`packages/ai/src/prompts/extraction-system.ts:149`). `zodToJsonSchema` omits it from `required`, but OpenAI strict structured-output mode requires *every* property in `required`. Every OpenAI attempt fails instantly: `400 Invalid schema for response_format 'oracle_structured_output': … Missing 'sensitivityReason'`. Gemini's structured mode doesn't enforce this, which is why only Gemini models pass. **Both OpenAI extraction models are categorically unusable** until this is fixed.

**M2 (gemini-2.5-flash via pool) — root cause (bug 2):** `packages/ai/src/providers/google-gemini-adapter.ts:167` hard-codes `AbortSignal.timeout(60_000)`. Extraction on this image-derived transcript exceeds 60s → aborts at exactly **60.1s** every time. (The prod *primary* is the **Vertex** variant `vertex_gemini_2_5_flash_extraction_primary` — a different adapter that works — but the admin-selectable pool id `google/gemini-2.5-flash` routes through the broken Google adapter.)

**CONTROL gemini-3.1-flash-lite — the only working extraction model.** 7 claims returned = 7 promoted, 0 rejections, 9.0s, **$0.003347**, ~$0.00048 per useful claim. Mix: 4 process_rule, 2 dependency, 1 exception_rule. All claims carry clean `[Rectangle:X] --()--> [Rectangle:Y]` edge quotes, e.g.:
- *(dependency)* "Mass production cannot commence until the PPS is approved by the licensor." — QUOTE `[Rectangle: PPS Approved] --()--> [Rectangle: Mass Production]`
- *(dependency)* "Factory pricing is only finalized after Sales confirms the buyer has approved the price." — QUOTE `[Rectangle: Price] --()--> [Rectangle: If Sales confirms buyer approves the Price]`
- *(exception_rule)* "If an audit fails, the process must revert to re-sampling." — QUOTE `[Rectangle: If Audit: Fail] --()--> [Rectangle: Re-sampling]`

## E.2 — Vision matrix (extraction held = flash-lite)

| Vision model | Result | Transcription | Edges | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **V1** qwen/qwen3-vl-235b-a22b-thinking | ✅ SUCCESS (6 runs) | 8593–10310 chars | 60–74 | only working vision model |
| **V2** google/gemini-2.5-flash | ❌ FAILED (run_cmqva5lx…) | — | — | thinking-level 400 |

**V1 qwen3-vl:** 17094 input tokens (fixed image), 3100–8825 output, 65–156s, $0.0125–$0.0241/run. High-fidelity swimlane format `[Lane: node]` + labeled edges; correctly captures named owners (Albert, Gina, Carlos, Junior designers). Best run: 10310 chars / 74 edges.

**V2 gemini-2.5-flash — root cause (bug 3):** `geminiThinkingConfig()` (`google-gemini-adapter.ts:64,105,220`) unconditionally attaches `thinkingConfig.thinkingLevel=MEDIUM` (from `default_vision_reasoning_effort="medium"`) to every Gemini call; 2.5-flash rejects it: `400 'Thinking level is not supported for this model.'`. Vision failed before any model_run was logged (captured in `documents.processing_error`). Confirmed gemini-2.5-flash is a multimodal *text* model — **no image-generation model was ever selected.**

## E.3 — Recommendation
**Extraction: `google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite`. Vision: `qwen/qwen3-vl-235b-a22b-thinking`.** These are the *only* models that actually complete in current prod, and flash-lite is also the cheapest extraction option ($0.25/$1.50 per 1M) at the best latency (9s) with full coverage (7/7 claims, 0 rejections). Quality is high and lane-accurate (see E.4).

**Prod left as (verified single-encoded, doc `complete`):**
- `default_extraction_route = "google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite"` *(changed from `google/gemini-2.5-flash`)*
- `default_vision_route = "qwen/qwen3-vl-235b-a22b-thinking"` *(unchanged)*
- `model_pool_extraction = [gpt-4o-mini, gemini-2.5-flash, gpt-4.1-mini, gemini-3.1-flash-lite]` *(all 4 kept per capability-enforcement requirement)*
- `enforce_model_capabilities = "true"` *(unchanged)*

**Why I moved the primary off gemini-2.5-flash:** the original "safe" config named 2.5-flash as primary, but 2.5-flash times out every run and only "works" by silently cascading to flash-lite. Naming flash-lite as the explicit primary is the **anti-masking** fix — the configured route now matches the model that actually runs.

## E.4 — Routing / anti-masking findings
1. **Silent fallback masking** is the core systemic risk: with a 4-model pool where only 1 model works, `default_extraction_route` is cosmetic — every run lands on flash-lite regardless. The bake-off was only possible by isolating pools to one model each.
2. **Lane accuracy independently verified** against both known ground truths in the final restore run (run_cmqva7at…, 7 claims): claim "PPS Audit … reviewed by **Gina** before sent to the factory" (bottleneck) == known *Gina/"Review Audit and send to factory"*; claim "**Carlos** is responsible for creating SKUs in DFlow/ColdLion/MasterData/ClickUp" == known *Carlos/"SKUs creation"*. Both confirmed correct. (Full pixel-level lane grading: not independently verified — repo `.env.local` service key is for a different project, so I could not mint a prod signed URL for the image.)
3. **Bug 4 (data):** `default_extraction_reasoning_effort` holds double-encoded JSONB `""` (literal two-quote string). Currently benign (`isReasoningEffort` rejects it → undefined) but should be cleaned to `"off"`.

**Out of scope / needs human:** chat, admin-alert-visual, PDF-attachment paths (cannot drive web UI); synthesis (0 brain_sections); broken `default_translation_route=qwen/qwen-mt-plus` left alone.

**Housekeeping:** all temp `_*.mjs` scratch files deleted (mine + prior agent's). The four code/data bugs are flagged as spinoff task `task_42c2055b` since fixing them requires a worker redeploy. Nothing committed or pushed.
